currentState: By the chapter-23 aftermath, Uther and Jaina stand in the tense space between witness trust and legal necessity. He does not exonerate her on sight, but he does protect her from the worse stupidity of the room and treats her report as essential truth.
powerDynamic: Uther owns formal authority once the responders enter the chamber. Jaina owns the most accurate understanding of what just happened and what Frostmourne still is. Their interaction works because each recognizes the other’s lane fast enough to prevent a second disaster.

physicalDynamics.pattern: Their key beat happens at the horse line: Uther turns to Jaina properly, gives her the lane in words, and Jaina answers with the smallest bow the chapter allows to count as gratitude.

Their chapter-23 contact is aftermath geometry: Jaina half-fallen and bloodied near the dais, Uther crossing the room fast enough to stop guards from mishandling her and to pull a report before panic wins.

nameUsage.utherCallsJaina: Command-sharp and direct in chapter23, treating her as witness, culprit-on-paper, and trusted source all at once.
nameUsage.jainaCallsUther: Stripped practical address. She gives him facts instead of appeal, which signals how much authority she still grants him even in chains.

history.event: Uther receives Jaina at Hearthglen as part of the incoming crisis team and leads her toward Arthas because her warning cannot stay outside the tent.
When Arthas refuses to wait, Uther backs Jaina’s source-plan lane openly and helps force the outer route clear for it.
Uther reaches the Imperial Chamber in time to forbid contact with Frostmourne and demand Jaina’s report before the room’s first false narrative fully hardens.
Jaina submits to Uther’s custody order because he is the one authority present who understands both the political danger of the room and the magical danger of the blade.

trajectory: Formal recognition under war pressure -> direct practical alliance around the Stratholme head-start run. -> chapter 23’s custody-and-witness alliance.

stateHistory.currentState: Mutual respect under battlefield pressure. Uther sees Jaina as the bearer of the one useful narrow answer left; Jaina receives his backing as practical grace rather than comfort.

Chapter 23 turns their earlier battlefield respect into ruined-chamber trust under custody. Uther arrives too late to save the king but early enough to recognize Jaina as the room’s key witness and operator, while Jaina trusts him enough to yield custody without trying to talk past the immediate danger.
